key_factors: France is ranked 1st in the official FIFA rankings as of July 2026, while Morocco has risen significantly entering the top 15.
France reached the quarter-finals after a 1-0 victory over Paraguay, while Morocco advanced by defeating Canada 3-0.
Morocco midfielder Ismael Saibari is officially ruled out with a hamstring injury, creating a confirmed key starter absence asymmetry in favor of France.

France traveled approximately 310 miles from Philadelphia to Foxborough, while Morocco traveled approximately 1,850 miles from Houston. This 1,540-mile travel difference exceeds the 800-mile threshold and warrants a 3-point adjustment in favor of France.
Sunny and warm conditions (high of 88°F) are forecast in Foxborough, affecting both teams equally (no weather asymmetry).
The referee is Argentine official Facundo Tello, who has issued an average of 3.5 yellow cards and 0.0 red cards per match in his 2 refereed games this tournament.
unverified_or_unknown: The physical fitness and availability of Aurélien Tchouaméni (groin injury) and Marcus Thuram (calf injury) for France are unconfirmed, as they are managing knocks and undergo late fitness tests.
The injury status of Morocco's Chadi Riad (knee issue) is unconfirmed, as his starting status is doubtful.
The exact starting lineups and tactical approaches are not officially confirmed at prediction time.

weather_check.conditions: Sunny, around 88°F, west-southwest winds 10-15 mph, no precipitation expected during the match
